session_client_like_config(3) Library Functions Manual session_client_like_config(3)

NAME

session_client_like_config

SYNOPSIS

#include <src/common/session/client_like.h>

Data Fields


session_client_like_run_fn run_fn
void * run_user_data
session_client_like_kind_t kind
void * discovery
bool(* custom_should_exit )(void *user_data)
void * exit_user_data
int max_reconnect_attempts
bool(* should_reconnect_callback )(asciichat_error_t last_error, int attempt_number, void *user_data)
void * reconnect_user_data
unsigned int reconnect_delay_ms
session_keyboard_handler_fn keyboard_handler
bool print_newline_on_tty_exit

Detailed Description

Configuration for session_client_like_run()

All media, display, audio, FPS, and snapshot settings are read automatically from GET_OPTION() inside the function. Callers only provide the fields below that differ per mode.

Definition at line 112 of file client_like.h.

Field Documentation

bool(* session_client_like_config::custom_should_exit) (void *user_data)

Optional additional exit condition. Called in the exit check logic alongside the global should_exit(). The loop exits when EITHER should_exit() OR custom_should_exit() returns true.

Use this for modes that need extra exit logic:

•
discovery: also exit when discovery_session_is_active() becomes false

NULL = use only the global should_exit().

Definition at line 153 of file client_like.h.

void* session_client_like_config::discovery

Active discovery session for this connection (opaque void *). When non-NULL, events will be processed periodically inside the shared exit check, and the session will be stopped during teardown. NULL for mirror mode and TCP client mode. Cast from discovery_session_t * when used (include src/discovery/session.h).

Definition at line 137 of file client_like.h.

Referenced by session_client_like_run().

void* session_client_like_config::exit_user_data

User data for custom_should_exit callback

Definition at line 156 of file client_like.h.

session_keyboard_handler_fn session_client_like_config::keyboard_handler

Keyboard handler for interactive controls (seek, volume, pause, help). Passed directly to session_render_loop(). NULL = no keyboard handling.

Mirror and discovery modes use session_handle_keyboard_input(). Client mode may pass NULL (server drives display).

Definition at line 216 of file client_like.h.

session_client_like_kind_t session_client_like_config::kind

Explicit client-like mode kind.

Definition at line 128 of file client_like.h.

Referenced by session_client_like_run().

int session_client_like_config::max_reconnect_attempts

Maximum reconnection attempts. Controls retry behavior: -1 = unlimited retries (keep trying forever) 0 = no retries (single attempt only, exit on failure) >0 = retry up to N times

Default: 0 (no retries). Client mode typically sets to -1. Discovery mode may set custom value based on role.

When run_fn returns non-OK, session_client_like_run() checks this to decide whether to retry or exit.

Definition at line 174 of file client_like.h.

Referenced by session_client_like_run().

bool session_client_like_config::print_newline_on_tty_exit

When true, write a bare '

Mirror mode: true. Client and discovery modes: false (server/host manages cursor).

Definition at line 229 of file client_like.h.

Referenced by session_client_like_run().

unsigned int session_client_like_config::reconnect_delay_ms

Delay in milliseconds before attempting reconnection. Applied after each failed attempt. 0 = no delay.

Can be a fixed value (e.g., 1000 for 1 second) or exponential backoff. Modes with backoff can use should_reconnect_callback to calculate delay based on attempt number.

Definition at line 203 of file client_like.h.

Referenced by session_client_like_run().

bool(* session_client_like_config::should_reconnect_callback) (asciichat_error_t last_error, int attempt_number, void *user_data)

Optional callback to determine if should attempt reconnection.

Called when run_fn returns with error code. Can implement custom reconnection logic:

  • discovery: return false when role changes to prevent retry
  • client: always return true (handled by max_reconnect_attempts)

Should return: true = attempt reconnection false = exit immediately

NULL = always attempt reconnection (unless max_reconnect_attempts reached).

Definition at line 190 of file client_like.h.

Referenced by session_client_like_run().
